title: See-Through Rectangles
description: Rectangles and opacity (transparency). Focus: what opacity means and how it affects overlapping shapes.

flashcards.definition: Opacity is how see-through something is. Low opacity = more transparent. High opacity = more solid.
flashcards.example:
# Opacity examples:
# 0   = fully transparent
# 100 = fully opaque (solid)

flashcards.definition: Transparent rectangles let you see shapes behind them.
flashcards.example:
Circle(200, 200, 80)
# then draw a semi-transparent Rect over it

flashcards.definition: A rectangle is positioned by its top-left corner and sized by width/height.

options: Where the shape is on the canvas
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: Opacity controls how see-through (transparent) a shape is.

quiz.prompt: If you lower opacity, what happens?
options: The shape changes into a circle
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: Lower opacity makes a shape more transparent (see-through).
